title: Quantitative Controls for Environmental and Agricultural Surveillance of Extended-Spectrum Beta-Lactamase Producing Escherichia coli
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ejll9","text":"The importance of standardized control strains for environmental antibiotic resistance (AR) efforts cannot be underestimated.  Control strains are useful in standardizing the quality of testing across laboratories and are essential for quality control of experimental procedures and reagents.  This protocol introduces two new agriculturally sourced Escherichia coli strains for use as environmental AR control strains in method development, surveillance, and research efforts.  Both strains are fully characterized, and whole-sequenced, and are available through multiple public sources.  ARS-C101 is a control strain that is extended-spectrum β-lactamase (ESBL) negative and tetracycline susceptible.  ARS-C301 is a control strain that is ESBL positive, and tetracycline resistant.  They are both compatible with the World Health Organization efforts: \"WHO integrated global surveillance on ESBL-producing E. coli using a “One Health” approach: implementation and opportunities” (aka \"Tricycle”). The current protocol describes how to make quantitative controls of ARS-C101 and ARS-C301 control strains using a modification of the WHO Tricycle protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":350,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":908,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"fk042","text":"Scope and Application","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hmbi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d13pq","text":"This protocol describes a method for the creation of quantitative controls, as developed and described by the World Health Organization, with minor modifications, as applied to two publicly available control strains.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7grp3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f9hha","text":"World Health Organization, 2021. \"WHO integrated global surveillance on ESBL-producing E. coli using a “One Health” approach: implementation and opportunities.” License: CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO. ISBN: 978-92-4-002140-2 https://www.who.int/publications-detail-redirect/9789240021402","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":87,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":215,"length":62}],"data":{}},{"key":"58q6k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"30na","text":"The original protocol for the steps described here can be found in section 5.3.6.8 of the WHO Tricycle protocol “Preparation of a quantitative positive control\".","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":67,"length":94},{"style":"italic","offset":67,"length":94}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9qq3r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eag4c","text":"This protocol describes the creation of quantitative controls from two Escherichia coli strains specifically identified for use as controls in the environmental and agricultural surveillance of both extended-spectrum β-lactamase producing and/or tetracycline resistant E. coli.  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":71,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":207,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":269,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"48pff","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1nfi9","text":"E. coli ARS-C101 is extended-spectrum β-lactamase negative and tetracycline susceptible, and E. coli ARS-C301 is extended-spectrum β-lactamase positive and tetracycline resistant.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":28,"length":1},{"style":"italic","offset":93,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":121,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"729mc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2rd2e","text":"Both strains are sequenced [JF1] [LD2], and available from the USDA-ARS culture collection and ATCC. The ARS-C301 strain is also available as a pre-made quantitative pellet, from Microbiologics.  See Materials section for further details on how to obtain the strains.  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":27,"length":5},{"key":2,"offset":33,"length":5}],"data":{}},{"key":"19nds","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e90u8","text":"This method is being developed as part of a United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service National Program 212 project “Managing Manure as a Soil Resource for Improved Biosecurity, Nutrient Availability, and Soil Sustainability”. steps: Wait until the end of the workday, and then pick an isolated colony of each control strain into 9 mL of growth medium (TSB). Incubate at 37 °C for 18:00:00 . "After one night of growth at 37 °C , there are approximately 1 X 10 9 CFU per ml in the broth, and the broth should appear visibly turbid" * *italicized text in quotes is taken directly from https://www.who.int/publications-detail-redirect/9789240021402

The following protocol is taken from section 5.3.6.8 of the WHO Tricycle protocol “Preparation of a quantitative positive control” , with minor modifications. World Health Organization, 2021. "WHO integrated global surveillance on ESBL-producing E. coli using a “One Health” approach: implementation and opportunities.” License: CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO. ISBN: 978-92-4-002140-2 https://www.who.int/publications-detail-redirect/9789240021402 Verbatim steps are indicated by " italic print in quotation marks" . World Health Organization. WHO integrated global surveillance on ESBL-producing E. coli using a "One Health" approach: implementation and opportunities Licence: CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO.. https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240021402 Escherichia coli ARS-C101 (negative control) is available from: the ARS Culture Collection https://nrrl.ncaur.usda.gov/ (Accession number: B-65681); ATCC (isolate ID BAA-3340). Escherichia coli ARS-C301 (positive control) is available from: the ARS Culture Collection https://nrrl.ncaur.usda.gov/ (Isolate ID: B-65682); from ATCC (isolate ID BAA-3341); and as a pre-quantified pellet (10-100 CFU) from Microbiologics (St. Cloud, MN) via a custom order (contact industrial microbiology QC products regional salesmanager https://www.microbiologics.com/contact-us by email requesting a quote forARS-C301). Safety The laboratory protocols described in this document involve culturing antibiotic resistant organisms and working with control strains that are known antibiotic resistant organisms. Procedures should only be performed in approved Biosafety Level 2 (BSL2) laboratories, following all BSL2-safety protocols. All work with the ARS-C301 positive control strain must be done using a biosafety cabinet.
"Prepare 10-fold serial dilutions: Take 1 mL of the overnight culture into 9 mL diluent and vortex to get a 10 -1 dilution. Repeat 4 times, yielding 10 -2 , 10 -3 and 10 -4 dilutions."

"Add 0.3 mL of the 10 -4 dilution to 29.7 mL sterile 40% glycerol and mix well. (This is a further dilution of 100X and thus a 10 -6 dilution). Incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature and mix again before proceeding."
"Add 0.1 mL of dilution 10 -6 that was prepared in step 6 to 0.9 mL sterile 40% glycerol and mix very well. (This is a 10 -7 dilution)."

Enumerate the 10 -4 dilution, 10 -6 dilution, and 10 -7 dilution. Note : Instead of standard plate dilutions, the method evaluation for the current protocol used Single-Plate Serial Dilution Spotting technique (Thomas et al., 2015). See citation below and this video link: SP-SDS: A Simple technique for microbial CFU enumeration - YouTube . Thomas, Pious, Aparna C. Sekhar, Rshmi Upreti, Mohammad M. Mujawar, and Sadiq S. Pasha.. Optimization of single plate-serial dilution spotting (SP-SDS) with sample anchoring as an assured method for bacterial and yeast cfu enumeration and single colony isolation from diverse samples. Biotechnology Reports 8 :45-55. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.btre.2015.08.003 Spot 20 µL each of the 10 -4 , 10 -6 , and 10 -7 dilutions onto the appropriate section of the enumeration plate. Single Plate Serial Dilution Spotting Template - resize to fit bottom of standard petri plate. CTX pos control (ARS-C301) – spot on TBX-CTX4. TET pos control (ARS-C301) – spot on TBX-TET32. Neg control (ARS-C101) – spot on TBX-Plain.
Incubate all plates at 37 °C Overnight .
Store the 10 -4 dilutions (one in diluent, one in glycerol), as well as the 10 -6 , and 10 -7 glycerol dilutions in the refrigerator Overnight .
Count the colonies in each plate section and calculate the CFU per 100 µL.
Make freezer stock aliquots of controls containing 100-120 colonies per 100 µL .

"If the 10 -6 dilution contained less than 100 colonies, use the 10 -4 dilution to prepare an appropriate stock containing 80-100 colonies per 100 µL using sterile 40% glycerol." "For example, if the 10 -6 dilution contained 50 colonies, dilute the 10 -4 dilution 50x (1 mL 10 -4 dilution + 49 mL sterile 40% glycerol."

Once dilutions containing 100-120 colonies per 100 µL are obtained, prepare 500 µL aliquots in cryogenic tubes for colony storage. Mix well, incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature , and mix again before freezing at -80 °C .
"Select four random tubes from each control strain, and thaw."
For each control strain, test all four tubes: Step Case 1: via Quanti-Tray Step Case 2: via Membrane Filtration or Standard Plate Count
Inoculate ARS-C101 and ARS-C301 onto individual TSA plates, streaking for isolation. Incubate at 37 °C Overnight .
Dilute 40 mL glycerol with 60 mL deionized water, and autoclave.

"If the 10 -6 dilution contained more than 120 colonies/100 µl, look at counts from the 10 -7 dilution. If the 10 -7 dilution contained 10-100 colonies, dilute the 10 -6 dilution with sterile 40% glycerol to reach 100 colonies per 100 µL ." "For example, if the 10 -7 dilution contained 80 colonies, dilute the 10 -6 dilutions 8X (5 ml 10 -6 dilution + 35 ml sterile 40% glycerol) to achieve about 100 colonies per 100 µL ."

Perform Colilert Quanti-Tray/2000 assay, in duplicate, according to the standard protocol using 100 µL of the enumerated E. coli stock for each tray. 1) Colilert plain 2) Colilert-CTX4 3) Colilert-TET32 For Colilert-CTX4, remove cap and add 100 µL of the 4 mg/mL cefotaxime stock solution to 99.9 mL of sterile water. Recap and shake prior to adding to the Quanti-Tray tray. For Colilert-TET32 add 100 µL of the 32 mg/mL tetracycline stock solution to 99.9 mL of sterile water. Recap and shake. For the negative control add 100 mL of sterile water. Recap and shake prior to adding to the Quanti-Tray tray. ARS-C101 (Neg control) – run using Colilert-Plain, Colilert-CTX4, Colilert-TET32. ARS-C301 (CTX & TET pos control) – run using Colilert-Plain, Colilert-CTX4, Colilert-TET32
Incubate all trays, cell-side-up, at 35 °C, +/-2°C for 24:00:00

Determine the counts of the quantitative controls. Counts need to lie within the range of the mean +/- 2x standard deviation. "If out of range, but within 3x standard deviations, investigate whether there was a reason for the deviating counts (i.e. control tubes stored for long times, and counts started to decline in earlier measurements." "If counts are not within 3x standard deviation of the mean, all measurements made that day need to be rejected."

Determine the counts of the quantitative controls. Counts need to lie within the range of the mean +/- 2x standard deviation. "If out of range, but within 3x standard deviations, investigate whether there was a reason for the deviating counts (i.e. control tubes stored for long times, and counts started to decline in earlier measurements." "If counts are not within 3x standard deviation of the mean, all measurements made that day need to be rejected."
Enumerate each of the four tubes for recovery on TBX-CTX4:

Standard Plate Count: For each of the four tubes, pipette 100 µL onto a TBX plate, 100 µL onto a TBX-CTX4 plate, and 100 µL onto a TBX-TET32 plate (total of four TBX plates, four TBX-CTX4 plates, and four TBX-TET32 plates). Spread evenly over the surface of the plate using a sterile loop or "hockey stick" spreading tool. Incubate overnight at 37 °C . See dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.xqhfmt6 for detailed instruction on how to quantify E. coli using the spread plate method.

Membrane Filtration: For each membrane, pipette 100 µL of sample into 99.9 mL PBS. Filter according to EPA Method 1604. For each sample place membranes onto a TBX plate, a TBX-CTX4 plate, and a TBX-TET32 plate (total of four membranes placed on TBX plates, four membranes placed on TBX-CTX4 plates, and four membranes placed on TBX-TET32 plates). Incubate overnight at 37 °C . See Method 1604: Total Coliforms and Escherichia coli in Water by Membrane FiltrationUsing a Simultaneous Detection Technique (MI Medium) for detailed instruction on how to quantify E. coli using the membrane filtration method.
If desired, take photos of the plates for laboratory notebook.
If desired, take photos of the Quanti-Tray trays for laboratory notebook.
If desired, take photos of the membranes for laboratory notebook.
